str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - beginner-coders
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Strategies for Dealing with the Frustration of Not Making Progress in Coding
entry.summary: In today's tech-savvy world, coding has become an essential skill to have. However, for beginners, it can be overwhelming and frustrating to make progress in this field. The learning curve is steep, and setbacks are a natural part of the process. This blog post aims to provide practical strategies that can aid in overcoming these hurdles. By breaking down complex tasks into smaller parts, focusing on learning fundamentals, practicing consistently, collaborating with others, utilizing resources, focusing on understanding concepts rather than just memorizing syntax, setting realistic goals, embracing mistakes and learning from them, taking breaks when feeling frustrated, and remembering that coding is a journey, progress becomes more manageable. These strategies help build confidence, mastery, and reduce frustration, making it easier to tackle more advanced tasks as you progress. Let's continue learning and growing together as coders!
